data.title: Marine Products outlines 2026 model year strategy amid moderating sales decline and stable inventory
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Marine Products Corporation (MPX) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Ben M. Palmer, President and CEO, reported that while "second quarter sales were down slightly compared to the prior year," the declines have moderated as production levels have stabilized. He said, "We are cautiously optimistic that the industry is working through excess inventory and that more certainty over model year 2026 pricing allows for better planning." Palmer highlighted the company's focus on positioning its brands for improved future demand, production efficiencies, and maximizing returns on investments. He noted a reduction in field inventory by 11% year-over-year and continued close partnerships with dealers who "remain cautious with regards to their levels of inventory."
  • Palmer confirmed excitement for the 2026 model year rollout, with "several portfolio-wide changes, also added some new products and refreshed a number of our models."
  • On tariffs, Palmer stated, "Tariffs remain top of mind. However, continued changes and ongoing negotiations make it very challenging to precisely plan at this point."
  • Michael L. Schmit, CFO, stated, "Sales were down 3% to $67.7 million, driven by a 13% decrease in the number of boats sold, partially offset by a positive 10% net increase in price and mix." Schmit added, "Quarterly sales have been stabilizing over the past few quarters as evidenced by the 15% sales increase over the first quarter of this year."
  • Schmit reported, "Gross profit decreased to $12.9 million, although our gross profit margin percentage of 19.1% was up 20 basis points from the prior year." He also noted, "SG&A expenses were $8.1 million in the quarter, up 9% or $0.7 million compared to last year's second quarter."
  • Palmer closed by affirming the company is evaluating "acquisition opportunities and other partnerships to enhance marine products portfolio" and believes the company's balance sheet and operational approach make it a "buyer of choice for high-quality assets."

Outlook

  • Palmer reiterated, "We see potential to deliver sales growth versus the prior year in the second half of 2025."
  • Schmit advised, "Our tax rate was 21.3% in the quarter and is likely to be slightly higher than that for the remainder of the year."
  • CapEx is expected to "pick up in the second half of the year and track between $2 million and $3 million for the year."
  • There was no explicit update to full-year revenue or EPS guidance, but management emphasized the importance of inventory management and the potential for industry improvement if interest rates decrease.

Financial Results

  • Schmit reported, "Sales were down 3% to $67.7 million," offset by a 10% net increase in price and mix, and a 13% decrease in units sold.
  • Gross profit was $12.9 million with a margin of 19.1%.
  • SG&A expenses were $8.1 million, up 9% from the previous year.
  • Diluted EPS was $0.12 in the second quarter, down from $0.14 last year.
  • EBITDA reached $5.6 million, down from $6.5 million in the prior year.
  • Operating cash flow year-to-date was $9.2 million, with free cash flow at $8.6 million.
  • The company ended the quarter with $50 million in cash and no debt.

Q&A

  • There were no analyst questions during the call. Operator confirmed, "There are no questions at this time."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts did not participate in the Q&A, so no sentiment or concerns were expressed.
  • Management's tone in prepared remarks was cautiously optimistic, with Palmer stating, "We are cautiously optimistic that the industry is working through excess inventory" and highlighting excitement for new product rollouts. Schmit maintained a measured tone, noting stabilizing sales and cost control.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from focusing on significant sales declines and uncertainty to a more stable outlook, emphasizing operational discipline and future growth potential.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management in Q2 noted that sales declines have moderated and highlighted a 15% sequential sales increase from the previous quarter, as opposed to Q1's emphasis on year-over-year sales being down 15%.
  • The focus shifted toward upcoming product launches and potential sales growth in the second half of the year, whereas the prior quarter was more concerned with managing through inventory and uncertainty.
  • SG&A expenses increased in Q2 compared to a decrease in Q1, and the tax rate was lower in Q2 at 21.3% versus 27.8% in Q1.
  • Both quarters emphasized careful inventory management and highlighted ongoing challenges with tariffs and interest rates.
  • Management’s confidence appeared to increase, with more emphasis on new product introductions and acquisition opportunities in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • Palmer highlighted macroeconomic uncertainty, specifically "tariffs, interest rates and the general economy."
  • He noted, "Tariffs remain top of mind. However, continued changes and ongoing negotiations make it very challenging to precisely plan at this point."
  • Supplier pricing for major inputs could change if tariffs are adjusted, creating further uncertainty for production costs.
  • Interest rates remain elevated, and while the market expects rate cuts, Palmer cautioned, "it may take some time for any rate relief to work through the industry and generate meaningful improvement in retail demand."
  • Dealers remain cautious regarding inventory levels.

Final Takeaway

Marine Products Corporation signaled stabilizing trends in its second quarter, with sales declines moderating and a notable sequential increase in sales. The company remains optimistic about its 2026 model year launches and continues to prioritize inventory management and operational efficiency, while actively seeking acquisition opportunities. Management highlighted ongoing risks tied to tariffs and interest rates, but expressed confidence in their strategic approach and financial strength to support future growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Rocket Pharmaceuticals cuts headcount, realigns pipeline
data.description:
Rocket Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:RCKT) announced on Thursday that it will reduce its workforce by about 30% as part of a restructuring effort.

This decision, along with other planned cost-saving initiatives, is expected to lower the company's operating expenses by nearly 25% in the 12-month period.

The restructuring will shift the company's focus towards its AAV cardiovascular platform, which includes treatments for Danon disease, PKP2-ACM, and BAG3-DCM.

Additionally, Rocket said it will concentrate on regulatory activities for Kresaldi.

As Rocket realigns its business and strategic priorities, it anticipates delays in the Fanconi Anemia and Pyruvate Kinase Deficiency programs. Consequently, the FDA approval for Fanconi Anemia treatment is now not expected until after 2026.

Rocket believes that its current cash reserves will sustain its operations through the second quarter of 2027.

data.title: PayPal, Wix.com expand partnership for unified payments experience to merchants
data.description:
PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) (NEOE:PYPL:CA) has expanded its partnership with the SaaS website builder platform, Wix.com (NASDAQ:WIX), for a unified payments experience to merchants.

The expanded partnership will see PayPal payment methods, like PayPal Wallet and PayPal Pay Later, integrated directly into the Wix Payments experience.

data.title: Jeff Bezos weighing possible purchase of CNBC business network - report
data.description:
Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos is evaluating a potential bid for business network CNBC after it's spun off from NBCUniversal parent Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA) later this year. 

An acquisition might make sense as it would add a "neutral voice" to his portfolio, which includes the Washington Post, according to a NY Post report on Wednesday, which cited an unidentified source close to Bezos.

Bezos hasn't approached Comcast regarding his potential interest, according to the report.

A representative for Bezos declined to comment to the NY Post.

In November, Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA) confirmed that it will pursue a tax-free spinoff of NBCUniversal’s portfolio of TV channels and list them as a separate public company, which will include USA Network, CNBC, MSNBC, Oxygen, E!, SYFY, and Golf Channel. The networks-focused entity that is being spun out of media and cable giant Comcas will be called "Versant."

data.title: Poland to finalize $4B U.S. loan guarantee for military upgrade
data.description:
Poland's Ministry of National Defense confirmed that Warsaw is preparing to secure a $4 billion U.S. loan guarantee to support the ongoing modernization of its armed forces, Reuters reported Thursday. The initiative is part of a broader effort under rising regional tensions and Russia’s war in Ukraine, aimed at reinforcing NATO’s eastern defenses.

Since 2022, the United States has extended more than $11 billion in loans and guarantees to Poland, enabling the acquisition of advanced systems such as Patriot air-defense batteries, HIMARS rocket platforms and Apache attack helicopters. This financial backing has significantly accelerated Warsaw’s efforts to modernize its military capabilities.

Poland has also become NATO’s highest spender relative to GDP, budgeting 4.7 percent of gross domestic product for defense in 2025 and committing to increase that figure to 5 percent by 2026.

data.title: Wall Street opens mixed after Tesla, Alphabet earnings, yields pop on jobless claims report
data.description:
The stock market opened mixed on Thursday, as investors digested multiple earnings reports, including Alphabet and Tesla earnings, and yields were higher after the initial jobless claims data came in hot. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.1%, the Dow (DJI) -0.7%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.3%

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.44%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 4 basis points to 3.93%.

The initial jobless claims fell more than expected in the past week.

Honeywell International (HON) shares fell 3% in premarket trade after the industrial conglomerate reported contracting margins and sluggish performance in key automation segments.

Wall Street's broader market averages finished higher on Wednesday after President Donald Trump announced a “massive” trade agreement with Japan and news circulated that the U.S. and EU are close to a trade deal as well.

"The risk-on tone has continued over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 at a fresh record thanks to growing optimism that more trade deals would be reached before August 1," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

So when it comes to the major economies, there’s now a deal with Japan, headlines pointing to one with the EU, and Bessent signalling a roll-over of the tariff reduction with China. And Trump announced a deal with Indonesia as well yesterday, Reid added. 

Trump will make a rare visit to the Fed on Thursday, the first by a sitting U.S. president in nearly 20 years. The visit is scheduled for 4 p.m. ET.

Tesla (TSLA) shares were down 8.7% as CEO Elon Musk said the company could face a "few rough quarters." The firm avoided a second consecutive top- and bottom-line miss, with profits in line with expectations.

Alphabet (GOOGL)(GOOG) stock was up 2.8% as the firm beat the expectations easily with another double-digit gain for revenues. 

Intel (INTC) will report on Thursday after the market close. 

The July PMI Composite Flash will land during market hours. The consensus looks for the PMI composite at 52.3 for July, versus 52.9 in the June final.

The new home sales report for June will also come in during market hours and is expected to recover somewhat but remain soft at 650K.

The Fed balance sheet will be released later in the day.

data.title: Zebra Ventures makes strategic investment in Xemelgo
data.description:
• Zebra Ventures, the corporate venture capital investment arm of Zebra Technologies (NASDAQ:ZBRA), on Thursday announced a strategic investment in Xemelgo, a cloud-based RFID software solutions.
  • This funding will support Xemelgo’s platform expansion into new verticals, enhance its AI capabilities, and empower its development team to meet rising demand.
